diagrams.title: Common Background AI Applications
diagrams.description: A visual overview of the various ways AI operates invisibly in the background of everyday technology, from email filtering to smart home devices. This helps learners recognize AI's pervasive but often unseen role in modern life.
elements: Central hub or network node representing 'Background AI'
Six application categories radiating outward: Email Spam Filtering, Voice Assistants, Recommendation Engines, Fraud Detection, Predictive Text, Smart Home Automation
Subtle dashed lines connecting applications to show they run 'behind the scenes'
User icons or touch points showing human interaction without seeing the AI
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrates the breadth and variety of AI applications that work invisibly in the background, helping learners appreciate how integrated AI already is in daily technology

diagrams.title: How Background AI Processing Works
diagrams.description: A flowchart showing the continuous cycle of background AI: data input → AI processing → intelligent output/action. Shows that this happens automatically without user awareness.
elements: Data input sources (user behavior, sensor data, transactions, search queries)
Processing block labeled 'AI Engine' with icons for machine learning, neural networks, pattern recognition
Arrow showing continuous loop (feedback loop) - AI learns from outputs
Output actions (filtered results, predictions, recommendations, automated responses)
Clock icon or circular arrows indicating continuous/24-7 operation
Shield or 'invisible' icon representing the hidden nature from users
diagrams.purpose: Explains the technical process of how background AI operates continuously, processing data and delivering intelligent outputs without explicit user commands

diagrams.title: Background AI in Technology Stack
diagrams.description: A layered architecture diagram showing where background AI fits within a technology system - from data sources through AI processing to user-facing applications.
elements: Bottom layer: Data Sources (databases, sensors, user行为 logs, external APIs)
Middle layer: AI Processing Layer (machine learning models, algorithms, data pipelines)
Top layer: User-Facing Applications (email clients, streaming services, banking apps, smart devices)
Vertical arrows showing data flow upward and intelligent actions flowing downward
Ghosted or faded appearance for the AI layer to represent its 'background' nature
Labels showing examples at each layer
diagrams.purpose: Provides a technical architecture view of how background AI integrates into broader systems, showing its role as an invisible enabling layer between data and user experiences
